LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IDA CHRISTINA FUA'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND ENTRY OF ORDER DIRECTING TURNOVER OF FUNDS HELD BY COURT UNDER C.C.P. 386, AND PAYMENT OF COSTS AND ATTORNEYS FEES UNDER C.C.P. 386.6, AND ORDER UNDER C.C.P. 386.1 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS PREMATURE. MR. SANCHEZ HAS YET TO APPEAR IN THE ACTION. FUA CITES PAR. 16 OF THE INTERPLEADER COMPLAINT TO ARGUE THAT SANCHEZ HAS NOT OBJECTED TO THE TENDER OF THE POLICY LIMITS. THAT PARAGRAPH DOES NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT MR. SANCHEZ HAS NO CLAIM TO THE FUNDS. FURTHER, PAR. 17 ALLEGES "THAT DEFENDANTS, AND EACH OF THEM, HAVE MADE CONFLICTING DEMANDS UPON CONTINENTAL FOR THE REMAINING LIMITS UNDER THE CONTINENTAL POLICY." CONTINENTAL NEEDS TO SERVE MR. SANCHEZ AND HE NEEDS TO APPEAR OR BE DEFAULTED BEFORE THE COURT DISBURSES THE FUNDS. THE COURT ENCOURAGES CONTINENTAL TO SERVE MR. SANCHEZ-EVEN IF NECESSARY BY PUBLICATION-TO AVOID RECEIVING AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FROM THE COURT'S TRIAL DELAY REDUCTION DEPARTMENT. THE COURT WILL ORDER THAT THE FUNDS BE DEPOSITED IN AN INTEREST BEARING ACCOUNT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332, BUS. TEL: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-12-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Ryan L. Dahm, Esq., of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC, appearing for Cross-complainant. Counsel informed the court of settlement of case. Court ordered case scheduled for an OSC re: CRC 1385 on 9/12/2017 at 10:30 am, in Department 610. Proceedings not reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Continued To Dec-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney Ryan L. Dahm of Cook Collection PCL (415-989-4730) appeared for defendant Ida Christina Fua moving party. No other appearances.

The Court adoped the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and cross complainant Ida Cristina Cruz Fua's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories Set One without objections is GRANTED. Defendants Yellow Cab Cooperative, INC and Taxi Equipment Leasing, LLC are Ordered to provide verified responses to the form Interrogatories within 20 days of this Order. The moving parties request for sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-01-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Entry Of Order Directing Turnover Of Funds Held By Court Under C.C.P. 386, And Payment Of Costs And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 386.6, And Order Under C.C.P. 386.1
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sherman C. Lee of Colliau Carluccio Keener et al (415-932-7000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Continental Insurance Co.

Attorney, Ryan L. Dahm of Cook Collection Attorneys (415-989-4730) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Ida Cristina Cruz Fua

Attorney, Robert S. Aaron of Aaron & Wilson, LLP (415-438-7804) appears telephonically for defendants, Yellow Cab and Taxi Equipment Leasing

The parties submit and the Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT IDA CHRISTINA FUA'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Entry Of Order Directing Turnover Of Funds Held By Court Under C.C.P. 386, And Payment Of Costs And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 386.6, And Order Under C.C.P. 386.1 is DENIED without prejudice as premature. Mr. Sanchez has yet to appear in the action. Fua cites par. 16 of the interpleader complaint to argue that Sanchez has not objected to the tender of the policy limits. That paragraph does not clearly show that Mr. Sanchez has no claim to the funds. Further, par. 17 alleges "that defendants, and each of them, have made conflicting demands upon Continental for the remaining limits under the Continental Policy." Continental needs to serve Mr. Sanchez and he needs to appear or be defaulted before the court disburses the funds. The court encourages Continental to serve Mr. Sanchez-even if necessary by publication-to avoid receiving an order to show cause from the court's trial delay reduction department. The court will order that the funds be deposited in an interest bearing account. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: John K. Stewart; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Aug-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
